Seed Launch Case Study:
Kami McBride
Module 6
Don’t be intimidated by this…
• Kami went all out
• She was an absolute newbie to this…
• Complete tech newbie
• This is about the most full-out Seed Launch you’re
ever going to see in a lot of ways
Module 3: Seed Launch Case Study
Overview
• Kami McBride: For almost 30 years, Kami has
been teaching people to grow and use herbs in
their daily lives for cooking, gardening, skin care
and stress reduction. She works to de-mystify the
world of herbal medicine, and loves helping
people learn how to use herbs in the home
setting for prevention and every day common
ailments.
• Email list: approximately 6,500 (built from
teaching at workshops, conferences and
colleges)

Overview
• Market/Niche: Health and wellness, family
wellness, children’s wellness, Mom’s wanting to
use natural remedies to take care of their family
• Product Name Created during Seed Launch:
Handcrafted Healing Herbal Oils - The simple,
homemade solution for non-toxic natural skin
care and the family friendly remedy for dozens of
common aches and pains, from burns and
bruises to eczema and muscle pain
Module 3: Seed Launch Case Study
Overview
• Offer price: $97
• Sales: 104 people purchased course with 0
refunds
• Total revenue: $10,088
Module 3: Seed Launch Case Study
Program Delivery
• She pre-recorded 3 videos before the class
started so people would have something to
begin with. She would release a few videos and
then take a survey.
• After each survey she organized all of the
questions and then did a live call answering the
questions that were submitted and also taking
live questions and comments on the calls.
Module 3: Seed Launch Case Study

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	3
Program Delivery
• “I took much longer to deliver the course than Jeff
talks about because people wanted more and I
kept making specific lessons that addressed all
the questions. This seed launch took place from
the beginning of May through the end of July.”
• “People gave me feedback that they wanted to
take breaks and fell behind, so we took catch up
breaks.”
Module 3: Seed Launch Case Study
Program Delivery
• “Because of the survey process I created a course
that was much more of what people wanted.
Because of all the feedback, I feel like this is a
much better course than what I could have made
on my own.”
• “Having taught this material for so long, I was
definitely in the wrong mindset, I thought I already
knew everything that people wanted. I was
actually REALLY surprised by how much I learned
about what people wanted and needed. I am glad
I learned this lesson now instead of creating a
bunch of courses without the feedback that you
get during a Seed Launch.”
